# Callouts & References {#sec-refs}

This chapter exercises callouts, display equations, and cross-references across the book.

## Callouts {#sec-callouts}

All five callout types use `callout-appearance: simple` (set in `_extension.yml`).
Icons are Font Awesome glyphs injected via the SCSS loop in `theme-base.scss`.

::: {.callout-note}
## A note callout.

{{< lipsum 1 >}}
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## A tip callout.

{{< lipsum 1 >}}
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
## A caution callout.

{{< lipsum 1 >}}
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## A warning callout.

{{< lipsum 1 >}}
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## An important callout.

{{< lipsum 1 >}}
:::

## Equations {#sec-equations}

Inline math: $y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x + \varepsilon$.

Display math with a cross-reference target (@eq-ols):

$$
\hat{\beta} = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y
$$ {#eq-ols}

## Cross-references {#sec-crossrefs}

Cross-references work across chapters in a Quarto book.

- Chapter: @sec-typography
- Section in another chapter: @sec-figure
- Figure: @fig-penguins
- Table: @tbl-species
- Equation: @eq-ols
- Citation: [TODO]

The cross-reference title delimiter is a literal dot (e.g. *Figure 2.1*), set via `crossref.title-delim: "\\."` in `_extension.yml`.
